steps.title: Spot the counterintuitive shift
steps.content: The key signal is “counterintuitive effect of _____ demand.” Normally, rising prices reduce demand—but here the opposite occurred: buyers who hadn't previously wanted cards suddenly thronged the market. So the blank must capture how rising prices unexpectedly created new demand.

steps.title: Match the signal to the choices
steps.content:
Elicit means 'draw out' or 'bring forth'—precisely what the passage shows when rising prices caused new demand from previously disinterested buyers.

The other three don't fit: capitalizing isn't idiomatic, exploiting describes leveraging demand rather than creating it, and satisfying means fulfilling existing demand, not generating new demand.

That matches choice C — C is correct.
